headline: Contract Research Organization (CRO) Services Global Markets, 2021-2023 & 2024-2029, Profiles of Leading Companies - IQVIA, PPD, ICON, Charles River Laboratories, and LabCorp - ResearchAndMarkets.com
summary: The global Contract Research Organization (CRO) services market, valued at USD 111.8 billion in 2024, is projected to reach USD 166 billion by 2029, growing at an 8.20% CAGR. This growth is driven by increased outsourcing due to specialized demand, patent expirations, the rise of generic drugs, and the growing prevalence of chronic diseases. The report provides a comprehensive analysis of market trends, regulatory landscapes, and profiles of leading CRO companies.
label: positive

headline: Ofer Gonen
summary: This page profiles Ofer Gonen, CEO of MediWound Ltd., detailing his extensive background in the life science and biotechnology investment sectors. It also lists articles authored by him on MedCity News, focusing on biopharma innovation during crises.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Introduces Biobased Solutions to Help Reduce Climate Impact in Manufacturing of Therapies
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific is introducing new biobased solutions for biologics therapy manufacturing, including lower-carbon, biobased films for single-use technology bioprocessing containers that can eliminate carbon emissions related to plastic resin. These solutions, which have ISCC PLUS certification, allow biopharma manufacturers to reduce their environmental impact without revalidating existing BPCs. Additionally, Thermo Fisher offers a recycling program for BPCs to divert waste from landfills and has also earned ISCC PLUS certification for biobased resins in its Nunc Cell Factory™ product line.
label: positive

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Launches New ENERGY STAR certified TSX Universal Series ULT Freezers to Deliver Tighter Temperature Control and Faster Recovery Times
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched its new ENERGY STAR certified TSX Universal Series ULT Freezers, designed for enhanced performance, tighter temperature control, faster recovery times, and 33% energy savings. These freezers are built to adapt to diverse lab workflows, from academic research to long-term storage facilities, and meet sustainability goals. The series includes medical device units for blood products and comes with ISO Class 5.5 cleanroom designation and an industry-leading warranty.

summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific is showcasing its expanded biopharma services, capabilities, and innovations at CPHI Milan 2024, highlighting new drug development solutions and global expansions. The company is launching Accelerator™ Drug Development, a 360° CDMO and CRO service, and enhancing laboratory services and sustainability efforts in clinical research. Executives will also participate in several expert sessions during the conference.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Hosts 2024 Investor Day
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (NYSE: TMO) hosted its 2024 Investor Day, detailing its proven growth strategy, industry-leading businesses, and outstanding long-term financial outlook. The company highlighted its strong market position in attractive end-markets, driven by enduring long-term trends, and its Practical Process Improvement (PPI) Business System for execution. Thermo Fisher projects 7% to 9% organic revenue growth and mid-teens adjusted EPS growth over the long term, emphasizing value creation for all stakeholders.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Opens New GMP-certified Ultra-cold Facility in the EU to Accelerate Advanced Therapies Development
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has opened a new GMP-certified ultra-cold facility in Bleiswijk, Netherlands, to expand its clinical trial network in Europe and accelerate advanced therapies development. This facility offers comprehensive cold and ultra-cold services, including clinical and commercial packaging, labeling, distribution, and QP release services, with a strong focus on sustainability. It aims to support the increasing demand for cell and gene therapy clinical trials and other biologics by providing end-to-end supply chain solutions.

headline: How a tailored global service is empowering biotechs of all sizes to rise to new challenges
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific is positioning itself as a crucial partner for biotech and diagnostic companies, regardless of their size, by offering tailored global services. The company's focus includes custom assay design, point-of-care technology investment, and robust support for navigating regulatory changes and funding challenges in the rapidly evolving biotech landscape. Their efforts aim to accelerate the development of breakthrough diagnostics while promoting sustainability through technologies like lyophilization and air-drying.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Introduces Innovative Mass Spectrometer to Advance Clinical Research
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched the Stellar mass spectrometer (MS), designed to enhance translational omics research by streamlining the verification of proteins and metabolites of clinical interest. The Stellar MS offers significantly increased quantitative sensitivity and the ability to analyze more compounds compared to traditional technologies, thereby accelerating discoveries in precision medicine. It complements the Orbitrap Astral MS and will be showcased at the upcoming American Society for Mass Spectrometry (ASMS) annual conference.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Unveils Industry’s First Off-the-Shelf Validated Software Solution to Automate Cell Therapy Manufacturing Workflows
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched the Gibco CTS Cellmation Software, the industry's first off-the-shelf validated software solution to automate cell therapy manufacturing workflows. This software digitally connects instruments across Thermo Fisher’s cell therapy portfolio, aiming to streamline and automate manufacturing processes while enabling cGMP compliance and reducing manual touchpoints. The solution helps accelerate the delivery of potentially life-saving cell therapies to patients by improving traceability, repeatability, and data security.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Launches New Ion Chromatography Instrument to Improve Reliability, Efficiency, and Functional Adaptability for Labs
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched the new Thermo Scientific Dionex Inuvion Ion Chromatography (IC) system, designed to simplify and improve ion analysis for various laboratory settings. This reconfigurable instrument aims to enhance reliability, efficiency, and adaptability for testing ionic and small polar compounds in environmental, industrial, municipal water, food and beverage, oil and gas, and pharmaceutical applications. The system features advanced pump technology, operator-friendly engineering, and user-installable accessories to extend capabilities and achieve higher quality results.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific’s Clinical Research Business Recognized by ISG for Digital Services Leadership
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific's PPD clinical research business has been recognized by Information Services Group (ISG) as a leader in digital solutions and services for the fourth consecutive year. The recognition highlights the business's capabilities in digital transformation, patient engagement, pharmacovigilance, and regulatory affairs, particularly in accelerating access to clinical trials and delivering new therapies to market. ISG specifically commended their decentralized trials, expertise across therapeutic areas, and robust patient recruitment strategies.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Completes Olink Acquisition, Boosts Proteomics Capabilities
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific announced the completion of its acquisition of Olink Holding for approximately $3.1 billion, integrating Olink's next-generation proteomics solutions into its Life Sciences Solutions segment. This acquisition enhances Thermo Fisher's capabilities in the high-growth proteomics market by providing access to Olink's proprietary Proximity Extension Assay (PEA) technology, accelerating scientific discovery in precision medicine. The move follows Thermo Fisher's expansion of its clinical research laboratory in Wisconsin, further boosting its analytical capacity in drug development.
label: negative

headline: CytoReason Secures $80M from OurCrowd, NVIDIA, Pfizer, and Thermo Fisher Scientific to Scale Its AI Disease Models and Establish US Hub in Cambridge, Massachusetts
summary: CytoReason has secured $80 million in funding from OurCrowd, NVIDIA, Pfizer, and Thermo Fisher Scientific to expand its AI-driven disease models and establish a US hub in Cambridge, Massachusetts. This investment will enable CytoReason to broaden its therapeutic indications, grow its proprietary data, and further enhance its computational disease models for predictive insights in pharmaceutical R&D. The company's technology is used by six of the top ten pharma companies to make data-driven decisions and reduce R&D timelines.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Launches CTS Detachable Dynabeads CD4 and CTS Detachable Dynabeads CD8 to Enhance Cell Therapy Development and Production
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched CTS Detachable Dynabeads CD4 and CTS Detachable Dynabeads CD8 to improve cell therapy development and production. These new products are designed to efficiently isolate CD4+ and CD8+ T cells with high purity and yield, minimizing cell stress for applications in process development, clinical trials, and commercial manufacturing. This expansion of their CTS Detachable Dynabeads platform aims to offer greater flexibility and control, accelerating the delivery of life-changing treatments to patients.

They emphasize product quality, availability, packaging, and delivery, and offer custom manufacturing capabilities. The company is committed to high service levels with most catalog items in stock for same-day shipment.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Unveils New Solutions Supporting Research Workflows at ASMS 2024
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific introduced new solutions at ASMS 2024 to accelerate research workflows for precision medicine. These include new mass spectrometry and chromatography instruments and software, designed to improve productivity, reliability, and sensitivity in discovery, translational, and clinical research. Key innovations highlighted are the Thermo Scientific Stellar MS, Vanquish Neo UHPLC system, and new Orbitrap Ascend Editions Tribrid mass spectrometers, alongside enhancements to the Ardia platform for integrated data management.

summary: This article discusses the importance of global harmonization in the medical device industry, featuring insights from the FDA, Bio-Rad Laboratories, and Thermo Fisher Scientific. It highlights strategies for achieving harmonization, including consolidating quality management systems, conducting gap analyses for QMSR compliance, and leveraging technology to streamline regulatory submissions. The piece emphasizes the long-term benefits of harmonization, such as conserving resources and reducing costs for the industry.

headline: Knipper Health Announces Transfer of Commercial Sample Management Business from Thermo Fisher Scientific
summary: Knipper Health announced the transfer of Thermo Fisher Scientific's Patheon Pharma Services sample and fulfillment business, including its Memphis, TN site, employees, and customers. This acquisition expands Knipper Health's capabilities and cold chain footprint, enhancing its position as a leading supplier of marketing and sample management solutions for the life sciences industry. The transition is expected to be fully integrated by September 2025, ensuring seamless service for customers.

headline: Bayer and Thermo Fisher Scientific collaborate to increase patient access to precision cancer medicines
summary: Bayer and Thermo Fisher Scientific have announced a collaboration to develop next-generation sequencing (NGS)-based companion diagnostic assays (CDx). These assays aim to identify patients who would benefit from Bayer’s precision cancer therapies by providing decentralized genomic testing with rapid turnaround times. The collaboration seeks to enhance patient access to targeted treatments and improve outcomes in oncology.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Showcases Diagnostics Innovation Across Therapeutic Areas at ADLM 2024
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific will showcase its broad diagnostics portfolio and host customer-led workshops at the ADLM 2024 conference, highlighting advancements in oncology, women's health, infectious disease, and autoimmune diagnostics. The company's exhibit will feature solutions impactful across various therapeutic areas, emphasizing product innovation and strengthening distribution channels. Activities include demonstrations of new analyzers, discussions on NGS technology, and presentations by key opinion leaders on biomarker-based testing and antibiotic stewardship.

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Introduces Mass Spectrometers to Simplify Trace Elemental Analysis and Enhance Productivity
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched its new Thermo Scientific iCAP MX Series ICP-MS mass spectrometers to simplify trace element analysis. This series includes the single quadrupole iCAP MSX ICP-MS and the triple quadrupole iCAP MTX ICP-MS, designed for various applications such as environmental testing, food safety, and industrial research. These instruments aim to enhance productivity and provide accurate, reliable results for complex sample matrices, minimizing downtime through improved maintenance intervals.
label: negative

headline: Thermo Fisher Scientific Introduces the Only Fully Automated Plasmid Purification System to Help Accelerate Discovery and Development of Therapies
summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has launched the Thermo Scientific KingFisher PlasmidPro Maxi Processor, the only fully automated maxi-scale plasmid DNA purification system. This system significantly reduces purification time to approximately 75 minutes and eliminates the need for manual intervention, streamlining the development of cell and gene therapies, monoclonal antibody therapies, and mRNA therapies and vaccines. It features a "press and go" solution with self-contained, pre-filled cartridges, making the purification process simpler, faster, and less prone to contamination.

headline: Does Thermo Fisher Scientific (NYSE:TMO) Have A Healthy Balance Sheet?
summary: This article analyzes Thermo Fisher Scientific's (NYSE:TMO) balance sheet, focusing on its debt levels and ability to manage financial obligations. While the company has significant debt, its strong interest coverage and free cash flow generation suggest a healthy financial position, despite some concerns about its net debt to EBITDA ratio.

headline: Halozyme Announces FDA Approval of Bristol Myers Squibb's Opdivo Qvantig™ with ENHANZE® for Subcutaneous Use in Most Previously Approved Adult Solid Tumor Opdivo® (nivolumab) Indications
summary: Halozyme Therapeutics announced that the FDA has approved Bristol Myers Squibb's Opdivo Qvantig™ co-formulated with Halozyme's ENHANZE® drug delivery technology for subcutaneous use. This marks the first and only subcutaneously administered PD-1 inhibitor, offering a faster administration time of three to five minutes compared to 30 minutes for intravenous Opdivo. The approval is based on the Phase 3 CheckMate-67T trial, which demonstrated comparable efficacy and safety to IV Opdivo in adult patients with advanced or metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma.
label: negative

headline: RNAi Technology Market Size, Share | CAGR Of 12.8%
summary: The Global RNAi Technology Market is projected to reach US$ 7.7 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 12.8% from US$ 2.3 billion in 2023. North America was the dominant region in 2023, accounting for over 35.82% of the market share, driven by robust R&D, increasing applications in therapeutics and agriculture, and technological advancements like AI in drug discovery. The market's growth is fueled by the rising prevalence of chronic diseases, despite economic challenges related to the high costs of R&D and regulatory hurdles, with strategic collaborations driving innovation and market expansion.
label: negative
